Title: Story Development GA Hardening & Launch
Description: Sprint 17 delivers the story development layer: beat board data model (Neo4j beats → scenes with arc assignment), arc CRUD (protagonist/antagonist/subplot), beat board UI (index cards on virtual corkboard), arc swimlane view, accounting export (EP Budgeting XLSX), Elasticsearch BM25/RRF tuning, faceted search, and cross-episode continuity timeline UI. Sprint 18 is GA hardening: separate production GKE cluster, multi-region DR (Postgres read replica, RPO < 5 min, RTO < 15 min), PagerDuty alerting, Tier 1 incident runbooks, Atlas migration rollback verification, Unleash P2 feature flags, y-redis migration (Hocuspocus → y-redis), pricing enforcement (Indie/Professional tiers), full SCIM provisioning for enterprise SSO, onboarding flow (< 5 min), empty state UX, in-app help, mobile-responsive web, and error states. GA milestone at Sprint 18 end (Week 38). Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprints 17–18.
Acceptance Criteria: Beat board UI: cards reorderable and linked to script scenes; Cross-episode continuity timeline rendered for a 3-episode test project; Accounting export produces valid EP Budgeting XLSX; Hybrid search tuned — Recall@10 improved ≥ 15% and faceted filters working; Production GKE cluster live with DNS configured; DR failover tested — RPO < 5 min and RTO < 15 min; Onboarding flow completed by new user in < 5 minutes; y-redis deployed and tested with 20 concurrent CRDT editors; All Tier 1 runbooks written and reviewed; GA milestone: full platform available

Title: Story Development — Beat Boards and Arc View
Description: Neo4j beat board data model: Beat nodes linked to scenes with beat type and arc assignment. Arc CRUD (protagonist arc, antagonist arc, subplot arcs). Beat board UI: index cards on virtual corkboard with drag-to-reorder. Arc swimlane view: character arcs visualized across story days. Cross-episode continuity timeline UI (full series view). Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 17; Wiki/03-data-model.md Bible Graph.
Acceptance Criteria: Beat nodes created in Neo4j with scene links; Arcs created with beat assignments; Beat board drag-to-reorder working; Arc swimlane rendered; Cross-episode timeline rendered for 3-episode test project

Title: Budget Accounting Export and Hybrid Search Hardening
Description: Budget Service: accounting export in EP Budgeting-compatible XLSX format. Elasticsearch hybrid search tuning: BM25 b/k1 parameters + RRF vector weight optimisation. Faceted search filters (episode, scene type, character). Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 17; Wiki/08-production-modules.md.
Acceptance Criteria: EP Budgeting XLSX validates against template; Recall@10 improved ≥ 15% on eval set; Faceted filters return correct scene subsets; Advanced search UI filters by character/location/episode/scene status

Title: GA Infrastructure — Production Environment and Disaster Recovery
Description: Separate production GKE Autopilot cluster from staging. Multi-region DR: Postgres read replica in secondary region. PagerDuty alerting (P99 > 1 s, error rate > 1%, disk > 80%). Tier 1 incident runbooks (DB failover, NATS outage, Temporal stuck workflow). Atlas migration rollback procedure verified. Unleash feature flags for all P2 features (default off). y-redis migration: Hocuspocus → y-redis for horizontal CRDT scaling. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 18; Wiki/12-infrastructure.md.
Acceptance Criteria: Production cluster live; DNS configured; Postgres read replica in secondary region; RPO < 5 min and RTO < 15 min verified; PagerDuty on-call rotation configured; Test alert fires and pages; Tier 1 runbooks reviewed by team; Atlas migration apply + rollback round-trip tested on staging; y-redis Deployment + HPA running; CRDT sync verified through y-redis with 20 concurrent editors

Title: GA Product Polish — Pricing Enforcement SCIM Onboarding and UX
Description: Pricing enforcement: Indie tier blocks AI generation beyond free quota; Professional token quota enforced. Full SCIM provisioning for enterprise SSO (Okta tested). Onboarding flow: new user → create org → create project → import script → invite collaborator in < 5 min. Empty state UX for all panels. In-app help: tooltips + keyboard shortcut cheat sheet. Mobile-responsive web (tablet breakpoint). Error states: graceful degradation when services unavailable. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 18; ADR-029 pricing.
Acceptance Criteria: Indie tier blocks AI generation at quota; Professional tier enforces token cap; SCIM endpoint creates/deactivates users from Okta; New user onboarding completed in < 5 minutes; All panels have empty states with CTA buttons; ? key opens shortcut reference; Core pages usable on iPad in portrait; API errors show toast notifications with no blank screens

Title: As a writer I can visualise my story structure on a beat board and track character arcs across the full series timeline
Description: Beat board and arc view provide visual story development tools in the web app. Beats link to script scenes; arcs span story days across episodes.
Acceptance Criteria: Beat board shows index cards for all beats in corkboard layout; Cards drag to reorder; Each card shows beat type arc and linked scene; Arc swimlane renders protagonist/antagonist/subplot arcs across story day axis; Cross-episode continuity timeline shows all story days from all episodes in one unified view; On-set assertions visible as pins on the timeline

Title: As a production accountant I can export the ScriptOS budget in EP Budgeting XLSX format and search for specific elements using faceted filters
Description: Accounting export and search hardening deliver the last P1 production planning features before GA.
Acceptance Criteria: EP Budgeting export: account codes map correctly; XLSX opens in Excel without errors; All cost lines present; Faceted search: episode filter returns only scenes from selected episode; Character filter returns only scenes featuring that character; Combined filters work correctly; Search results ranked by relevance with BM25 + vector RRF

Title: As a DevOps engineer the production environment is live with disaster recovery tested and monitoring in place before GA
Description: Production infrastructure must be completely separate from staging with verified DR and full observability before GA.
Acceptance Criteria: Production GKE cluster provisioned and DNS pointing to production; Postgres read replica replicating lag < 30 s; DR failover drill: primary shutdown → read replica promoted → traffic restored within RTO; PagerDuty alert fires within 60 s of P99 threshold breach; All Tier 1 runbooks stored in team knowledge base and reviewed; y-redis handles 20 concurrent CRDT editors without data loss; P2 Unleash flags all in off state on production

Title: As an enterprise admin I can provision and deprovision users automatically via SCIM and team members complete onboarding in under 5 minutes
Description: Enterprise readiness and new-user experience polish are GA requirements for commercial launch.
Acceptance Criteria: SCIM /Users endpoint: POST creates user; PATCH deactivates; tested with Okta SCIM connector; Pricing tiers: Indie account AI generation blocked at 50 generations/month; Professional quota enforced; New user test: org create → project create → FDX import → invite sent in < 5 min; All empty states have actionable CTAs; Error state: API unavailable → toast shown; no spinner loops or blank pages; Mobile: script editor and scene list usable on 768 px viewport

Title: Implement Beat board data model in Neo4j: Beat node (id uuid type beatType title description storyDayId arcId sceneId orderIndex); implement BeatArc node (id type name characterId); implement arc CRUD gRPC in Bible Service; NATS event bible.beat.created on beat creation
Description: Beat nodes created and linked to scene via HAS_BEAT relationship; Arc nodes linked to beats; getBeatsForProject(projectId) returns ordered beat list; createArc and assignBeatToArc mutations work

Title: Build beat board GraphQL subgraph extension in Bible Service: Beat and BeatArc types; createBeat updateBeat reorderBeats deleteArc mutations; beatsForScript and arcsForProject queries; resolver wires to gRPC
Description: createBeat mutation returns Beat; reorderBeats accepts [{ beatId orderIndex }] list; arcsForProject returns arcs with nested beats; beats field added to Scene type in Script subgraph via @key federation

Title: Build beat board UI: virtual corkboard canvas (react-dnd or @dnd-kit) with index cards; card shows beatType arc title and scene link chip; drag-to-reorder calls reorderBeats mutation; new card form in slide-over panel
Description: Cards draggable; order updates persist; Scene link chip navigates to scene in editor; Card colour coded by beat type (setup/confrontation/resolution); Empty state shows + Add First Beat prompt

Title: Build arc swimlane view: horizontal swimlane per arc across story day axis; beat cards placed at story day positions; cross-episode continuity timeline view calling timelineView(projectId) API showing story days across all episodes with assertion pins
Description: Arc swimlane renders correctly for a 3-arc test project; Cross-episode timeline shows story days from 3 episodes in chronological story-day order; Assertion pins clickable to show assertion detail

Title: Continuity Service: implement timelineView(projectId) gRPC method returning all StoryDay nodes across episodes sorted by story day number with assertions and violation counts; extend GraphQL subgraph with continuitySummary(projectId) query
Description: timelineView returns story days in correct order across 3-episode test project; continuitySummary returns violation count per story day; response time < 200 ms for 100 story days

Title: Budget Service: implement accountingExport(budgetId) generating EP Budgeting-compatible XLSX; map ScriptOS account codes to EP Budgeting account code schema; write to S3 presigned URL; expose via GraphQL mutation exportToEPBudgeting
Description: Exported XLSX opens in Excel without errors; Account codes present in correct columns; Fringes calculated; Summary totals match line totals; Download completes within 10 s for 500-line budget

Title: Elasticsearch hybrid search tuning: run offline eval harness on 200-query golden set; tune BM25 b (0.3–0.8) and k1 (0.8–2.0) parameters and RRF vector weight; implement faceted aggregations (episode scene_type character status); add faceted filter UI in advanced search panel
Description: Recall@10 improves ≥ 15% vs baseline on golden set; Episode facet counts match scene distribution; Character facet returns only scenes with that character speaking; Combined facets AND-compose correctly

Title: DevOps: provision production GKE Autopilot cluster separate from staging; configure DNS and TLS (cert-manager); deploy all 15 services to production with production-grade resource limits; run smoke test suite against production after deploy
Description: kubectl get nodes returns 3+ nodes in production cluster; All 15 services healthy (readinessProbe passing); Smoke test: create project → import FDX → publish saga completes → watermark verified; DNS + TLS certificates valid

Title: DevOps: provision Postgres read replica in secondary GCP region via Cloud SQL cross-region replica; configure connection string failover in app config; run DR drill (primary shutdown → manual failover → RTO measurement); document RPO/RTO results
Description: Read replica replication lag < 30 s under normal load; DR drill: failover completes within 15 min (RTO); data loss < 5 min (RPO); Connection string ENV updated to point to replica after failover; Results documented in runbook

Title: DevOps: configure PagerDuty integration in Prometheus Alertmanager; define alert rules (P99 > 1 s
Description: error_rate > 1%
Priority: pod crash loop); write Tier 1 runbooks for DB failover / NATS outage / Temporal stuck workflow / cert expiry; verify Atlas migration rollback procedure on staging
State: Test alert fires and pages on-call within 60 s; 4 Tier 1 runbooks in team knowledge base; Atlas rollback: apply migration → observe error → rollback → schema matches pre-migration state

Title: y-redis migration: replace Hocuspocus with y-redis Deployment (y-redis image) + HPA (min 2 max 10 replicas) + y-redis Worker Deployment; update Collaboration Service to connect to y-redis instead of Hocuspocus; configure Redis CRDT stream key prefix; load test 20 concurrent editors for 30 min
Description: y-redis Deployment running; HPA scales on CPU > 60%; CRDT sync works for 20 concurrent editors on same document without data loss; Loro state converges after network partition simulation; Hocuspocus deployment removed

Title: DevOps: create Unleash feature flags for all 13 P2 features (table-read-tts native-player location-scouting multilingual-export pitch-deck-gen marketplace mobile-app-ios budget-actuals callsheet-pdf-v2 search-semantic-v2 ai-storyboard watermark-digimarc legal-marketplace); all flags default off in production environment
Description: 13 flags created in Unleash production environment; All default off; SDK integration verified: toggling flag in Unleash UI reflects in app within 30 s; No P2 feature visible to any user without explicit flag enable

Title: Auth Service: implement full SCIM 2.0 provisioning endpoint (/scim/v2/Users and /scim/v2/Groups); support POST (create) PATCH (update/deactivate) GET (list/filter) per RFC 7644; test with Okta SCIM connector; pricing enforcement middleware: Indie tier blocks AI mutations beyond monthly quota and Professional tier enforces token cap via Redis counter
Description: SCIM POST creates user and sends welcome email; SCIM PATCH with active=false deactivates user and revokes JWT; SCIM GET with filter=userName returns correct user; Okta test app provisions and deprovisions user; Indie quota block returns 402 with quota_exceeded error; Professional token cap enforced via Redis sliding window

Title: Frontend: implement full onboarding wizard (4 steps: org create → project create → script import → invite collaborator); empty state components for script list / scene list / breakdown panel / schedule / budget; in-app help system (? shortcut opens keyboard cheat sheet modal); mobile-responsive CSS (768 px tablet breakpoint) for editor and dashboard; error state components (API error toast with retry; no blank screens)
Description: New user completes 4-step onboarding in < 5 min in usability test; All empty states render with CTA buttons pointing to first action; ? key opens help modal showing all shortcuts; Editor usable on 768 px viewport (no horizontal scroll); Any GraphQL error shows toast with message; No blank white screens on any route error
